decisions.summary: Partial fix already landed on main (commit 31ef04dda) — index.html now has a dark, theme-aware boot shell ('Connecting…') plus a bundle-independent boot watchdog that reloads once the server's index.html AND its referenced bundle are serveable again. That fully covers fix-direction #2 (no blank/white pre-mount document during a restart). This issue's REMAINING scope is fix-direction #1 plus the 5-step implementation plan in the 2026-06-07 issue comment: keep the already-mounted app alive and reconnect IN-APP rather than tearing it down with a full reload.

Liveness uses an app-level heartbeat on the /ws/rpc domain-events stream, NOT a raw ws.ping(). /ws/rpc is Effect-managed (RpcServer.toHttpEffectWebsocket, ws-rpc.ts:892) so there is no raw socket to ping. The server Stream.merges a periodic { type: 'system.heartbeat' } item into subscribeDomainEvents; the client treats any inbound frame (heartbeat or real event) as proof of life.

The heartbeat is added as a new SystemHeartbeatEvent variant in the @panctl/contracts DomainEvent union so it decodes on the wire. The client reducer needs NO change: applyEvent's default case already does `void (event as never); return state`, so an unrecognized type is ignored without throwing or mutating state. The heartbeat is liveness-only.

The heartbeat carries NO real event-store sequence. EventRouter.handleEvent classifies events by `sequence` via the recovery coordinator; a heartbeat must be intercepted by type and early-returned BEFORE classifyDomainEvent so it never corrupts the snapshot/replay sequence machinery. It only resets the staleness watchdog.

Root-cause fix: narrow RootErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch (recovery.tsx) so a GENERIC (non-module-load) render crash no longer calls window.location.reload(). Only true module/asset-load errors reload (the legitimate 'new hashed chunk after rebuild' case). Generic crashes keep the document mounted and fall through to the in-app manual fallback / reconnect overlay. This reverses the regression from 3345c7c11.

Snapshot-fallback cliff (EventRouter SNAPSHOT_FALLBACK_WINDOW_MS, currently a silent give-up after 3 min) is explicitly LOW PRIORITY per the issue comment. Scope it minimally: when the window expires without a successful bootstrap, surface the actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' overlay (reuse the overlay from the overlay-wiring bead) instead of silently stopping.

hazards.summary: Heartbeat must not pollute the sequence-based recovery coordinator. If a heartbeat reaches EventRouter.classifyDomainEvent it could be (mis)read as a real sequence and trigger a spurious replay or be deferred forever.
hazards.mitigation: Intercept `event.type === 'system.heartbeat'` at the very top of handleEvent: reset the watchdog and return immediately. Do NOT push it into pendingBatch. Add a unit test asserting a heartbeat frame neither calls applyEvents nor advances coordinator state.

hazards.summary: Heartbeat must only be merged into subscribeDomainEvents (the main app stream). subscribeIssueEvents / subscribeAgentOutput are filtered views; injecting heartbeats there would leak liveness frames into per-issue consumers and possibly fail their filters.
hazards.mitigation: Apply Stream.merge ONLY in the subscribeDomainEvents handler (ws-rpc.ts WS_METHODS.subscribeDomainEvents). Leave the other stream handlers untouched.

hazards.summary: wsTransport has TWO reconnect paths: Effect.retry(Schedule.fixed(retryDelay)) inside `run`, and the onExit handler that calls resetTransport() + setTimeout(run, 1000). A backoff change that only touches one leaves the other hammering.
hazards.mitigation: When replacing Schedule.fixed with capped exponential + jitter, account for both paths. The Effect.retry schedule is the primary target; document or align the onExit fixed 1000ms delay so the two paths don't fight. Verify with fake timers.

hazards.summary: Watchdog false-positives. A healthy-but-idle connection produces no real events; without the heartbeat the ~35s staleness timer would fire on a perfectly good socket and force needless reconnects.
hazards.mitigation: Watchdog threshold (~35s) MUST be >= 2x the server heartbeat interval (~15s) so a single dropped beat never trips it. Build the watchdog only AFTER the heartbeat is emitting (bead ordering enforces this). Test with fake timers: 1 missed beat = no reset; 2+ missed = reconnect.

hazards.summary: Narrowing the generic-crash auto-reload could, in theory, leave a genuine post-restart chunk/data-mismatch render crash showing only the manual fallback instead of self-healing. The in-app heartbeat+watchdog+overlay reconnect is the intended replacement for that auto-heal, but a crash that has already unmounted React cannot reconnect in place.
hazards.mitigation: Accept the manual 'Reload dashboard' fallback for true render crashes (matches 'reserve full reload for asset 404'). The asset-404 self-recovery in installRecovery AND the index.html boot watchdog still cover the real restart-asset case. Keep isModuleLoadError reload path intact; only remove the generic-crash branch.

hazards.summary: Retry-delay / backoff / watchdog tests must not use real timers (project rule: real retry delays across many tests cause OOM and force maxForks:1).
hazards.mitigation: All backoff and watchdog tests use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(). Existing EventRouter.test.tsx and recovery.test.ts are the precedents to follow.

hazards.summary: packages/contracts is a separate build artifact. Adding the heartbeat variant requires rebuilding contracts (cd packages/contracts && npm run build) before the server or frontend will see it; a stale dist causes confusing 'unknown event' or type errors downstream.
hazards.mitigation: The contracts bead's acceptance criteria include a successful `npm run build` in packages/contracts. Downstream beads (server, client) depend on it via blocks edges.

sessionHistory.note: Auto-mode planning session for PAN-1580. Discovered that fix-direction #2 (boot shell) already shipped on main via 31ef04dda; scoped remaining work to the in-app heartbeat/watchdog/overlay reconnect plan plus the generic-crash auto-reload root-cause fix.
